title: Calliandra anomala
taxonomy: Calliandra anomala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Calliandra, which comprises about 120 species of plants. The genus Calliandra is further grouped under the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. This subfamily contains a wide range of plants with pea-like flowers. The classification of Calliandra anomala reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.

intro: Calliandra anomala is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the powderpuff tree due to its unique powderpuff-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calliandra anomala include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Calliandra anomala is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments, for the treatment of various health conditions.
